question: Why is the IF bseg_xref1 = space guard commented out in YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:998? When was it removed, by whom, and why?
why_unknown: No git blame on YRGGBS00 source. Only the SROCHA 2010 comment marker visible elsewhere in the file.
follow_up: Check TADIR / E071 transport history for YRGGBS00 changes. Or ask UNESCO ABAP team directly.
notes: YRGGBS00 version history via SE38 version comparison. H48 scope.
answer_partial: Confirmed via code read (YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:998): line `* if bseg_xref1 = space.` IS commented out. UXR1 therefore unconditionally overwrites bseg_xref1 with USR05.Y_USERFO whenever the user has that param, silently losing any user-typed value. ASYMMETRY DISCOVERED: UXR2 (line 1026) STILL has the guard `IF bseg_xref2 = space` — only UXR1 is unguarded. This is the root cause of manual XREF drift observed in DQ-019 (21,754 Q1 2026 edits).
still_unknown: Exact transport that commented the IF bseg_xref1 = space guard. Narrowed to I_KONAKOV transports 2009-2010. To definitively close: SE38 diff between D01K951442 and its predecessor D01K951407 (also KONAKOV, 2009-08-27 different hour) on lines 996-1016.
answer_update: Transport history via E071 + E070 extracted: 57 transports modified YRGGBS00 between 2002-2026. Main editor: I_KONAKOV (~20 transports spanning 2008-2023, including the 2015-2017 era when substitution mods were heavy). Other editors: MAGAL/PARENT-DU (2002 initial), AHOUNOU (2003-2006), MENARD (2022-2026), RIOS (2025). CLUSTER of substitution-related transports: D01K951442 (2009-08-27 'WF - FI substitutions user-exit correction' by KONAKOV), D01K951618 (2009-08-27 'WF - FI Substitution rules' by KONAKOV), D01K960319 (2010-04-26 'WF - FI substitutions' by KONAKOV). The guard was most likely commented in one of these KONAKOV transports. Definitive identification requires diff-by-diff compare of YRGGBS00 source between transport versions (VRSD returned TABLE_WITHOUT_DATA via RFC — would need SE38 Version Management GUI).

question: Where is the substitution step-to-prerequisite linkage stored at UNESCO? GB905 and GB921 are both empty via RFC_READ_TABLE broad probe.
why_unknown: Standard SAP GGB1 expects step prerequisites in GB905. They are empty at UNESCO P01 (Session #051 probe). Step prerequisites must be implicit via naming convention OR stored in a mechanism not exposed through RFC.
follow_up: GGB1 GUI inspection in SAP (SE16N on GB9* won't show more — needs GGB1 transaction). Or check if SUBST ABAP code directly calls UXR* from an enhancement point that bypasses GB905.
notes: Check GB01 / GGB1 tables via RFC + GCTAB/GCSTR secondary tables. H48 scope.
answer: Substitution step prerequisites at UNESCO ARE DECLARATIVE via 3-table chain: GB922 (step definition: SUBSTID+SUBSEQNR+CONDID implicit via exit name) -> GB921 (step-to-CONDID linkage: 16 rows for SUBSTID='UNESCO') -> GB901 (boolean expressions indexed by CONDID, 583 rows total, 56 rows for BOOLID LIKE '3UNESCO%'). My prior session #054 claim that prerequisites were 'procedural in FORM bodies' was WRONG — they are declarative. Correction: the FORM body contains the ACTION logic once the declarative prereq passes. KEY FINDING: step 005 (UXR1/XREF1) has NO prerequisite row in GB901 — fires UNCONDITIONALLY for every BSEG line. Step 006 (UXR2/XREF2) has a prerequisite: `HKONT IN ('1375012','1375022','1375032','1375052','1341011','1341021')` — only fires for specific GL accounts. GB905 exists but returned 0 rows for UNES-related BOOLIDs.
findings_location: Gold DB: GB922 (17 UNES rows), GB901 (583 rows with BOOLEXP), GB903 (3 rows); YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt FORM bodies
tables_architecture: GB922 = step definition. GB921 = step-to-condition linkage (MANDT+SUBSTID+SUBSEQNR -> CONDID). GB901 = boolean expression body (BOOLID+SEQNR -> BOOLEXP multi-line text). GB903 = boolean header (BOOLID -> SHORTNAME+SETID). GB931 = VALIDATION step-to-condition for VALID='UNES' (12 rows, separate from substitutions). GB02C = message class mapping.

question: Why does F110 substitution fire on the vendor clearing line but NOT on the bank GL line, while F-53 fires on both? Empirically observed asymmetry with unknown mechanism.
impact: MEDIUM - explains F-53 vs F110 asymmetry
why_unknown: Could be (a) F110 uses BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST which bypasses callpoint 3, (b) a step-level filter on F110 BLART='ZP', (c) F110 payment method program creates the bank GL line post-substitution. Without GB905 or GGB1 GUI access, can't distinguish.
follow_up: Inspect F110 payment program (SAPF110S) for explicit XREF handling. Or test with a non-HQ F110 poster (currently impossible at UNESCO — only 2 F110 users both in HQ).
notes: Empirical ST05 trace of F110 vs F-53 substitution callpoint firing in TS3. Requires live test posting. H48 scope.
scope: UXR1 has NO filter by TCODE/BUKRS/callpoint context within the FORM body. Both UXR1 and UXR2 are registered as callpoint-3 (complete document) substitutions for UNES. Therefore the F110 vs F-53 asymmetry cannot come from the FORM itself. Two remaining hypotheses: (a) F110 batch context iterates BSEG lines differently, applying substitution only to vendor lines (SAP standard behavior with bseg-lifnr filter inside the callpoint dispatcher), (b) GGB1 or GB921 callpoint-level prerequisites filter tcode but are not extracted to Gold DB. Definitive answer needs ST05 SQL trace in TS3 with one F110 run + one F-53 posting to capture the actual substitution call sequence per BSEG line.
blocks_on: Live SAP session in TS3 — user-assisted work required. Cannot proceed autonomously via RFC.
enrichment: New data reduces the asymmetry mystery but does not fully explain F110 vs F-53. Finding: UXR1 has NO declarative prereq (fires on every BSEG line of every UNESCO posting). UXR2 has declarative prereq HKONT IN (bank-related GL list). So XREF1 auto-write should fire for EVERY line in EVERY tcode (F110, F-53, FB60, MIRO, etc.). The asymmetry observed in INC-000005240 (F110 fires on vendor clearing line but not bank GL line) must therefore come from either (a) SAP standard F110 batch context only iterating certain lines through callpoint 3, or (b) a downstream filter on bseg-hkont within substitution dispatcher. ST05 trace still required to resolve.

If yes, are Ophelia STEPHENSON-ODLE (PERNR 10136066) and Yanhong ZHANG (PERNR 10098989) authorized signatories on any of those other cartons? They are currently active in SAP RY 50010054 (UIS signatures for all transfers) but not on the 2026-04-02 Citibank Canada carton.
why_unknown: SAP BCM responsibility groups are entity-level, not bank-account-level. One group 'UIS signatures for all transfers' covers every UIS bank account at every bank. We only have the Citibank Canada carton on file. Without all UIS cartons we cannot distinguish legitimate multi-bank coverage from stale SAP config.
blocks_action: SAP cleanup delimit for STEPHENSON/ZHANG on UIS groups
notes: Query bank masterdata T012K for UIS company code + compare to signatory panel. User question, awaits response.

question: Is the role split on UIS BCM rules intentional? Anssi YLI-HIETANEN (10097358, Treasurer) is on rule 90000004 (Commit) but not on rule 90000005 (Validation). Lamin SANNEH (10150918) is on rule 90000005 but not on rule 90000004. The Citibank Canada carton lists both flatly as 'authorized to sign jointly two by two' without distinguishing validator vs committer. Is the SAP split deliberate (e.g., Treasurer commits only) or a maintenance accident?
why_unknown: The carton does not carry SAP rule semantics. The business rule for the validator/committer split is oral/undocumented and must be confirmed by TRS.
blocks_action: Any role-mirroring cleanup on UIS BCM panel
answer: The role split was a maintenance drift, NOT intentional. TRS confirmed by authorizing the full cleanup on 2026-04-13: all 8 signatories on the Citibank Canada carton are now on BOTH rule 90000004 (Commit) and rule 90000005 (Validation). 'Sign jointly two by two' means any two authorized signatories can sign together — no validator-only or committer-only split by design. Sanneh was added to Commit, Yli-Hietanen was added to Validation.
notes: ANSWERED session #052 (see `answered_session` field). Role split is intentional per OOCU_RESP structure.

question: Did transport D01K9B0BN9 (2024-03-26, N_MENARD, 52 objects) carry YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_AE and _BH classes to P01, and if so why does TADIR probe today show them as NOT FOUND?
context: Transport-intelligence classification of D01K9B0BN9 lists CLAS objects YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_AE + YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_BH + YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_DE + YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_FR (along with ENHO Y_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_* spots). Phase 0 Finding D (claim chain) ran TADIR probe in P01 and reported AE/BH classes do NOT exist in P01. Possible explanations: (a) D01K9B0BN9 was never imported to P01 (only stayed in D01); (b) classes were deleted or renamed in a subsequent transport; (c) TADIR probe used wrong client/system; (d) the transport entries are class-shells that were never activated and got cleaned up. This matters because if AE/BH logic exists somewhere, the structured-address V001 design has to consider it; if truly absent, no impact.
how_to_resolve: Three checks: (1) RFC E070 / E071 in P01 for D01K9B0BN9 → status code R (released) confirms import; (2) RFC TADIR in P01 for OBJ_NAME=YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_AE / _BH → present/absent; (3) if absent, check E071 for any subsequent DELE entry against AE/BH class names.
evidence_added_session_63: Gold DB cts_objects queried 2026-04-30: AE/BH classes have 1 cts entry each (only D01K9B0BN9 2024-03-26). DE/FR/IT/FALLBACK/UTIL have 2-4 entries each (re-touched). AE/BH never re-touched = consistent with "created in D01 but never imported to P01 OR imported then deleted". RFC TADIR probe still pending (VPN down 2026-04-30). Priority MEDIUM → LOW (V001 design excludes AE/BH).

question: For each historical DMEE-tree transport (R3TR DMEE / PAYM/* objects), which specific DMEE_TREE_NODE entries were created / modified / deleted? E071 only captures the tree-level object, not node-level diff.
context: Session #63 transport-intelligence reading of the 44-transport cohort gave us PGMID + OBJECT_TYPE + OBJECT_NAME granularity. For ABAP transports (CLAS/METH/ENHO/FUNC) this is sufficient — we know the exact method/class touched. For DMEE tree transports (R3TR DMEE PAYM/<tree>), the granularity stops at the tree level: we cannot say from E071 whether a transport added new nodes, modified existing node sources, changed conditions, or deleted nodes. The DMEE_TREE_NODE.AS_TIME / VERSION fields on each row would let us reconstruct per-transport node-level diffs by comparing snapshots at transport release time.
how_to_resolve: Three options: (1) RFC_READ_TABLE DMEE_TREE_NODE for each tree filtering by AS_TIME between adjacent transport release times — labor-intensive but reproducible. (2) Use SAP standard DMEE Tx version-history viewer (if active version snapshots are kept) — manual GUI step. (3) Replay each transport in a sandbox and capture the before/after diff — high cost. For V001 design this granularity is NOT blocking: surgical-pattern claim 102 + tree-level classification claim 101 are sufficient to confirm the operational pattern. Add this KU only if a Phase 4 regression surprises require historical node-level forensics.

question: What is the minimum SAP authorization profile required to execute Tx DMEE menu Tree -> Versions -> Create Version / Activate Version / Deactivate Version?
context: Session adding SAP-standard DMEE/PMW reference doc (knowledge/domains/Payment/dme_creation_payment.md). UNESCO has never bumped a DMEE tree past VERSION=000 (claim 102 TIER_1, all 44 historical transports). The 2-file + DMEE-versioning strategy in dmee_versioning_procedure.md requires Create/Activate/Deactivate Version operations, none of which the team has exercised. Some SAP installations lock these menu actions behind extra auth profiles beyond standard DMEE display/edit. M_SPRONK + N_MENARD need to know before Phase 2 Week 0 dry-run.
how_to_resolve: Three converging probes: (1) Web search SAP-side for auth object F_DMEE_NDP (Format Tree Definition) activities 01/02/03/06 plus S_TABU_DIS for DMEE_TREE_*. (2) SU24 readout in P01 (or D01) for transaction DMEE -> default check-objects + activities. (3) Empirical: M_SPRONK runs the menu in D01 sandbox; if it fails, SU53 trace identifies the exact missing object. Output: 1-page auth requirements checklist appended to dmee_versioning_procedure.md.

question: When Tx DMEE menu Tree -> Versions -> Create Version copies V000 to V001, are all 26 DMEE_TREE_HEAD columns copied verbatim, or do any (HANDLE_BYTES, XMLEMPTYELEMENTPROCESSINGTYPE, CHARSET, XSLTDESC, etc.) get default-reset?
context: dmee_versioning_procedure.md documents the canonical 3-step procedure (Create / Edit / Activate) but does not specify header-field semantics on copy. DMEE_TREE_HEAD has 26 columns including XML-behavior controls (XMLEMPTYELEMENTPROCESSINGTYPE, HANDLE_BYTES) that materially affect output. If any of these silently default-reset on V001 creation while V000 had non-default values, V001 output could diverge from V000 in non-obvious ways even before any node edits. Risk: bank-rejection or empty-element regression that surfaces only post-cutover.
how_to_resolve: Two probes (run cheap one first): (1) Empirical, LOW cost: in D01 sandbox dry-run on Z_DMEE_VERSIONING_TEST tree (already planned in dmee_versioning_procedure.md), populate all 26 header fields with non-default values, execute Create Version, then RFC_READ_TABLE DMEE_TREE_HEAD WHERE TREE_ID=<test> for both V000 and V001 -> diff = authoritative answer. (2) Source-code, MEDIUM cost: ADT pull the SAP-std FM/method that performs version copy (likely DMEE_VERSION_CREATE or method on CL_DMEE_TREE) -> read the field-by-field copy logic. Output: annotated 26-column copy-map appended to dmee_versioning_procedure.md.

question: Are DMEE V001 changes packaged as Customizing transports (request type W) or Workbench transports (request type K)? Tree node edits are typically Customizing, but version create/activate may flag Workbench.
context: Phase 2 transport-ID assignment table (dmee_versioning_procedure.md) lists 8 transports for V001 cutover, all marked TBD on type. Pre-allocating the wrong transport type forces a re-creation cycle and risks a partial release. SAP-side: DMEE format trees are listed under R3TR DMEE / PAYM<tree> in TADIR, but the request-type binding depends on the development class (TDEVC) and object-class flag.
how_to_resolve: Two probes: (1) Single RFC call: SELECT KORRDEP, OBJ_NAME FROM TADIR WHERE PGMID='R3TR' AND OBJECT='DMEE' AND OBJ_NAME='/SEPA_CT_UNES' (or any UNESCO tree) -> KORRDEP value tells us C (Customizing) vs S (System/Workbench). (2) Empirical (preferred, captures version-operation specifics): on D01 sandbox dry-run of Z_DMEE_VERSIONING_TEST, observe the actual transport-prompt UX for each of Create / Edit / Activate -> SAP shows the request type when you save. Output: one-line confirmation added to dmee_versioning_procedure.md transport table.

question: What does the body of SAP KBA help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/fiaccounting/3361880828.html (PMW: General Information and DMEE/DMEEX, parent 3361880730) actually say beyond what secondary-source synthesis covers?
context: User shared the URL with a screenshot of the VIA PMW topic tree (ACH Formats, Checks, Country specific formats, Format IDOC, Modifying Standard PMW Formats, PMW formats: Codepage, PMW formats: Customizing, PMW: General Information and DMEE/DMEEX, Payment Advice Creation, Payment formats supported in S/4 HANA and in ERP, SEPA and CGI Format Tree). The page is a JS-rendered SPA -> WebFetch + curl both return only the HTML title shell, no body. The dme_creation_payment.md section synthesizes the same VIA PMW topic from public secondary sources (SAPinsider, SAP help Customizing Payment Methods for DMEE, AUMTECH, Techlorean, ERProof) and covers ~95% of DMEE concepts. The remaining 5% may carry the exact UNESCO-relevant gotcha that prompted the user to open this KBA.
how_to_resolve: Only resolvable by user: (1) Log into SAP Support / SAP for Me with the user's account, (2) Open KBA 3361880828, (3) Paste the body text (or screenshot) into the conversation. Once received, integrate any new facts into knowledge/domains/Payment/dme_creation_payment.md section 8 and any specific notes (e.g. 1947527) under knowledge/domains/Payment/sap_standard_reference/sap_notes/. Cost: 2 minutes for user, zero for agent. Until then, this KU stays open and the secondary-source synthesis is treated as authoritative-but-incomplete.
